entries.title: Previewing GPT-5.6 Sol: a next-generation model
entries.raw_summary: OpenAI previews GPT-5.6 Sol, a next-generation model with stronger capabilities in coding, science, and cybersecurity, paired with its most advanced safety stack.

entries.title: How agents are transforming work
entries.raw_summary: A new OpenAI research paper shows how AI agents are transforming work, enabling longer, more complex tasks and expanding productivity across roles.

entries.title: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil LLM-optimized inference chip
entries.raw_summary: OpenAI and Broadcom introduce Jalapeño, a custom AI chip built for LLM inference to improve performance, efficiency, and scale across AI systems.

entries.title: Helping build shared standards for advanced AI
entries.raw_summary: OpenAI helps build shared standards for advanced AI, supporting evaluation frameworks, safety practices, and global cooperation through the Appia Foundation.

entries.title: How GPT-5 helped immunologist Derya Unutmaz solve a 3-year-old mystery
entries.raw_summary: GPT-5 Pro helped solve a 3-year-old immunology mystery, offering insights into T cell behavior. The breakthrough could support cancer and autoimmune research.

entries.title: How Omio is building the future of conversational travel
entries.raw_summary: Discover how Omio uses OpenAI to power conversational travel experiences, accelerate product development, and transform into an AI-native company.

entries.title: Patch the Planet: a Daybreak initiative to support open source maintainers
entries.raw_summary: OpenAI introduces Patch the Planet, a Daybreak initiative helping open-source maintainers find, validate, and fix vulnerabilities with AI and expert review.

entries.title: Daybreak: Tools for securing every organization in the world
entries.raw_summary: OpenAI introduces new Daybreak tools, including Codex Security and GPT-5.5-Cyber, to help organizations find, validate, and patch vulnerabilities at scale.

entries.title: Codex-maxxing for long-running work
entries.raw_summary: Learn how Jason Liu uses Codex to preserve context, manage complex projects, and help work continue beyond a single prompt.

entries.title: Samsung Electronics brings ChatGPT and Codex to employees
entries.raw_summary: Samsung Electronics deploys ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to employees worldwide, marking one of OpenAI's largest enterprise AI rollouts.

entries.title: Introducing computer use in Gemini 3.5 Flash
Import AI 462: Superpersuasion; self-sustaining AI; paths to ASI
entries.raw_summary: AI systems are reliably more persuasive than expert humans at changing minds on policy and donations. Research shows AI outperformed professional fundraisers by 3x at raising money. The newsletter also covers self-sustaining AI timelines and DeepMind's analysis of paths to superintelligence.

entries.title: GLM-5.2 is the step change for open agents
entries.raw_summary: Z.ai's GLM-5.2 represents a significant capability leap for open-weight models, matching proprietary systems in coding and reasoning tasks. The release follows Claude Fable 5's ban and demonstrates that frontier-class performance is achievable by open labs with fewer resources, raising important questions about AI regulation and open model accessibility.

entries.title: STARK raises €500M to build Europe's next defense prime
entries.raw_summary: German defense company STARK secured €500 million funding to manufacture unmanned strike systems. The capital will fund production lines across Germany, UK, and Ukraine, with focus on affordable, software-defined autonomous platforms like Virtus loitering munitions that can be assembled in minutes.

entries.title: How Revolut runs AI at scale
entries.raw_summary: Revolut's machine learning engineering leader discusses operating AI systems across 70 million customers in 40+ countries. The fintech manages generative AI through a single gateway with unified governance, fallback chains, cost controls, and mandatory human oversight for critical decisions affecting users.

entries.title: Odyssey raises $310M Series B for world models
entries.raw_summary: Odyssey announced $310M Series B at $1.45B valuation for developing interactive world models. The company launched Starchild-1 for real-time multimodal generation, Agora-1 for shared multi-agent environments, and PROWL for self-directed model improvement through reinforcement learning.

entries.title: Macrodata: Robots need a data refinery
entries.raw_summary: Macrodata, founded by creators of FineWeb dataset, raised $4M pre-seed to build data infrastructure for robotics. The company's Refiner tool processes multimodal physical data from diverse robot formats and sensors into training-ready datasets, supporting the scaling of embodied AI systems.

entries.title: 刚刚，DeepSeek V4更新DSpark，推理速度提升80%
entries.raw_summary: DeepSeek V4推出了投机解码框架DSpark，同步开源了全栈推测性解码框架DeepSpec。DSpark已部署在真实线上流量中，大幅加速了大语言模型推理速度，与前一代部署相比，生成速度分别提升60%-85%（Flash模型）和57%-78%（Pro模型）。

entries.title: ECCV 2026 | 视频生成模型真的会推理吗？303道题全面揭示世界模型的推理短板
entries.raw_summary: MME-CoF-Pro基准包含303个图像-文字-视频推理样本，涵盖16个推理类别。研究发现主流视频生成模型推理能力普遍不强，最强的Veo也仅56分，推理能力与生成质量几乎完全解耦。文字提示是双刃剑，虽提升推理分数却损害一致性。

entries.title: 陈天奇新书上线：面向ML系统的现代GPU编程
entries.raw_summary: CMU助理教授陈天奇发布免费在线书籍《Modern GPU Programming For MLSys》。内容聚焦高性能GPU kernel编写，主线围绕Blackwell架构展开，以矩阵乘法和FlashAttention为实战案例。

entries.title: 大神Karpathy用Claude的方式，原来是这样的？
entries.raw_summary: 社区流传了一份据称是Andrej Karpathy实际使用的CLAUDE.md文件。该文档强调在编写新代码前应先阅读现有代码库，理解项目风格和模式，避免生成与项目不符的代码。核心原则包括保持简单、进行外科手术式修改和充分验证。

entries.title: State of AI Report 2025
entries.raw_summary: Annual analysis of key AI developments across research, industry, politics, and safety. Key findings include OpenAI's continued frontier lead, China's DeepSeek emergence as credible #2, reasoning breakthroughs, scientific AI collaboration, and mainstream adoption with 44% of U.S. businesses now paying for AI tools.

entries.title: 2025 Report Launch Blog Post
entries.raw_summary: Detailed blog post covering the State of AI Report 2025 findings, including insights on industrial-era AI infrastructure, political shifts toward 'America-first AI', and the transition of safety research toward pragmatic concerns around reliability and governance.
